topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The attic air hung thick and dusty, smelling of aged paper and forgotten lavender. Liam had been tasked with clearing out his grandmother’s belongings, a chore he’d dreaded. Tucked inside a steamer trunk, beneath moth-eaten linens, he found it: a meticulously organized binder. Every single assignment from primary school through high school, color-coded, dated, and filed with unnerving precision. His hands trembled as he lifted the heavy book. It wasn't just the volume of work that caught his attention; it was the sheer, unwavering dedication embedded in the system. He flipped through the pages, a cold knot tightening in his stomach. Who had done this? And why? A sudden, sharp urge to close the book and flee washed over him, making his breath catch in his throat.

[story 2]

“Mom, what is all this?” Sarah asked, her voice thin and hesitant. She was sifting through a box of old school papers, and beneath faded permission slips and dried-out report cards, she uncovered a thick ledger. It contained every essay, every math worksheet, every poetry submission from her entire academic career. A wave of profound unease washed over her, making her skin prickle. She traced a name—her own name, written in looping script—and felt a strange hollowness bloom in her chest. She quickly shoved the ledger back into the box, her movements jerky, avoiding the weight of the evidence. She couldn't look at the contents again; the sheer completeness of it felt suffocating.

[story 3]

Mark found the box hidden behind a stack of outdated textbooks. It was labeled simply, "Schoolwork." Inside, the collection of completed assignments was staggering. From awkward kindergarten drawings to complex college research papers, it was an exhaustive chronicle of his academic life, preserved perfectly. He sat on the floor, the silence of the room amplifying the frantic thumping in his ears. He picked up a history essay from sophomore year, the ink still crisp. He stared at the neat handwriting, a profound sense of disorientation settling over him. He felt unsteady, as if the ground beneath him had shifted, and he couldn't bring himself to move, paralyzed by the strange, heavy secret he now held.
